steps.action: Create isolated Org A and Org B owner accounts, onboard both orgs, and seed one person, one care plan, and one care note in each org within the round3-auditcross-20260401 namespace.
steps.expected: Each org has distinct tenant-scoped records available for validation.
steps.observed: Org A and Org B were onboarded successfully, and fixture records were inserted only inside the assigned namespace.

steps.action: Authenticate as the Org B owner and call GET /api/persons, GET /api/care-plans, and GET /api/care-notes.
steps.expected: Each list response contains only Org B records.
steps.observed: All three endpoints returned 200 and listed only Round3 AuditCross Person B, Round3 AuditCross Plan B, and Round3 AuditCross note B for org B.

steps.action: Probe known Org A identifiers from the Org B session (GET /api/persons/7fc78fbe-318d-4389-930b-15d3fd0315df, GET /api/care-plans/4486ea80-a224-40b0-a933-bc9f7a70c14f, and GET /api/care-notes?personId=7fc78fbe-318d-4389-930b-15d3fd0315df).
steps.expected: Cross-tenant requests are denied or hidden and expose no Org A data.
steps.observed: The direct person lookup returned 404 Not Found, the direct plan lookup returned 404 Not Found, and care notes queried with the Org A personId returned 404 Person not found.

evidence.network: GET /api/persons -> 200; GET /api/care-plans -> 200; GET /api/care-notes -> 200; GET /api/persons/7fc78fbe-318d-4389-930b-15d3fd0315df -> 404; GET /api/care-plans/4486ea80-a224-40b0-a933-bc9f7a70c14f -> 404; GET /api/care-notes?personId=7fc78fbe-318d-4389-930b-15d3fd0315df -> 404

assertions.title: Immutable audit trail

steps.action: Open /round3-auditcross-20260401-org-a/audit-log as the Org A owner.
steps.expected: The central audit log loads and exposes immutable audit entries.
steps.observed: The page rendered successfully with audit entries, an “Entries are immutable” badge, and no browser errors.

steps.action: Update the Org A organisation name in Settings, then re-open the audit log and expand the latest entry.
steps.expected: A new audit entry shows timestamp, user identity, action type, entity reference, and before/after field values.
steps.observed: The newest entry showed the owner name, action Update, entity type Organisation, entity reference 8af9c43b…, and an expanded before/after diff for the name field.

steps.action: Attempt to exercise create/update/delete flows for care-significant records through the real UI surface.
steps.expected: Person, care plan, or daily-note mutation surfaces exist so their audit generation and immutability can be verified end-to-end.
steps.observed: The foundation UI does not expose those mutation surfaces yet: People is only a stub page, and /round3-auditcross-20260401-org-b/care-plans plus /round3-auditcross-20260401-org-b/daily-notes both return 404, so create/update/delete audit coverage for care-significant records could not be fully tested.

evidence.network: GET /round3-auditcross-20260401-org-a/audit-log -> 200; org settings update rendered a new audit entry in the central log.

assertions.issues: The central audit page now works for organisation create/update entries, but the real UI still lacks accessible care-significant create/update/delete surfaces, so the assertion cannot be fully verified against persons, care plans, or daily notes.

assertions.title: Audit trail search and filtering

steps.action: Open the central audit log for Org A and inspect the available controls.
steps.expected: The central log is searchable/filterable by user, entity type, action type, and date range.
steps.observed: The page exposed Search, Action, Entity type, From, and To controls, but no user filter control was present.

steps.action: Apply the Action=Update filter and inspect the result ordering.
steps.expected: Filtered results remain reverse-chronological and usable.
steps.observed: Filtering worked and the single update entry stayed newest-first.

steps.action: Look for an entity-level audit surface from the implemented entity routes.
steps.expected: Entity detail pages expose an audit/history tab in addition to the central log.
steps.observed: No entity-level audit tab was reachable through the real UI: the care-plans route returned 404 and the organisation settings page had no embedded history tab/section.

assertions.issues: The central audit page lacks the required user filter, and no entity-level audit tab was available from the implemented foundation routes.

frictions.description: Foundation does not yet expose person/care-plan/daily-note mutation UIs, so minimal tenant fixtures had to be seeded directly inside the assigned namespace before validating API isolation.
frictions.resolution: Seeded one person, one care plan, and one care note per org using a temporary Bun script scoped to round3-auditcross-20260401, then validated isolation only with those records.

blockers.description: Care-significant write surfaces needed to fully verify immutable audit coverage are still absent from the foundation UI (People is stubbed; care-plans and daily-notes routes 404).
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Checked the real dashboard navigation plus direct org-scoped URLs for care-plans and daily-notes; the routes remained unavailable.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: VAL-CROSS-007 passed, VAL-CROSS-015 is blocked by missing care-significant mutation surfaces, and VAL-CROSS-016 failed because the central audit log still lacks a user filter and no entity-level audit tab is reachable.
